```json
{
  "isFinal": true,
  "references": [
    {
      "chunk": "Links:\n1. [Manager Configuration](https://flamenco.blender.org/usage/manager-configuration)\n2. [Sleep Schedule | Flamenco](https://flamenco.blender.org/usage/worker-configuration/sleep-schedule/)\n3. [Shaman Storage System](https://flamenco.blender.org/usage/shared-storage/shaman)\n4. [Frequently Asked Questions](https://flamenco.blender.org/faq)\n5. [Shared Storage](https://flamenco.blender.org/usage/shared-storage)\n\n# Flamenco Blocklist Configuration\n\nBased on the Flamenco Manager documentation, the blocklist is configured farm-wide in the &#96;flamenco-manager.yaml&#96; configuration file.[1]\n\nThe key blocklist-related settings are:\n\n- **&#96;blocklist_threshold&#96;**: This parameter controls when a machine is added to the blocklist. In the example configuration, it's set to &#96;3&#96;, meaning a worker is blocklisted after failing 3 times.[1]\n\n- **&#96;worker_timeout&#96;**: Set to &#96;1m&#96; in the example, this defines how long before a worker is considered unresponsive.[1]\n\nTo disable the blocklist farm-wide, you would modify the &#96;blocklist_threshold&#96; value in your &#96;flamenco-manager.yaml&#96; file. Setting it to a very high number or removing the threshold would effectively prevent workers from being blocklisted based on failures.\n\nThe configuration file is located next to the &#96;flamenco-manager&#96; executable, and changes take effect when Flamenco Manager is restarted.[1]",
      "title": "Web search results for query: Flamenco render farm disable blocklist machine farm-wide configuration 2025"
    }
  ],
  "rejected": false
}
```
